untranslatable_reason: Signal requires explicit trend line detection (identifying higher lows in uptrend/lower highs in downtrend, counting touches, measuring slope consistency across touches). Standard trend line construction and validation cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators and operators. The signal depends on geometric line analysis and manual point identification that exceeds the capabilities of price-action indicators. Pivot points used as proxy, but this does not capture the core requirement of trend line methodology with touch counting and slope consistency validation.
translation_notes: This signal is fundamentally about trend line methodology—a manual charting technique requiring identification of swing points, line drawing, and touch counting. The allowed indicator set lacks explicit trend line objects and geometric validation operators. While pivot points provide some trend reference, they cannot substitute for the specific trend line construction and consistency checks described in the signal. A proper implementation would require: (1) trend line construction algorithm, (2) touch-counting logic, (3) slope consistency measurement, and (4) penetration buffer definition—none of which are available in the current indicator/operator framework.
